name: Maunawili
desc: Lush valley behind Kailua — large lots, tropical landscaping, mountain trails, peaceful and green with a rural feel.
insider: Maunawili Falls Trail is one of the most popular hikes on Oahu — go early on weekdays to beat the crowds and parking crunch
The valley gets more rain than Kailua town — everything is dramatically green and lush but mold is a real home maintenance issue
Lots are larger than typical Kailua — you'll see horses, fruit trees, and big tropical gardens on multi-acre parcels
The Maunawili Demonstration Trail is a 10-mile ridge-to-ridge hike with incredible Ko'olau views — advanced hikers only
Wild pigs roam the upper valley — they can tear up yards and gardens, so fencing is essential if you're growing anything
The valley dead-ends with no through traffic — it's remarkably quiet and private for being so close to Kailua town
Maunawili Falls pool is a popular cliff-jumping spot but rocks are slippery and injuries happen — exercise real caution
Old Pali Road connects through Maunawili and is a gorgeous walking and cycling path closed to cars
The neighborhood has a rural, horse-country feel — many properties have agricultural zoning that allows livestock
Flash flooding happens on Maunawili Stream — the falls trail crosses the stream multiple times and becomes impassable in heavy rain
